headline: Bangsa Malaysia Concept Mentioned Zero Times in 12th Malaysia Plan
context: The Bangsa Malaysia (Malaysian Nation) concept, introduced by Mahathir in 1991 as Vision 2020's core aspiration, does not appear in the 12th Malaysia Plan (2021-2025).

cards.big: Bangsa Malaysia was the centrepiece of Vision 2020.
cards.sub: The 12th Malaysia Plan does not mention it once. The aspiration has been quietly abandoned.

cards.big: Vision 2020 defined Bangsa Malaysia as a nation 'not defined by ethnicity.'
cards.sub: The 12th Plan uses 'Keluarga Malaysia' (Malaysian Family), which does not carry the same integrationist meaning.

cards.big: Ethnic identification on government forms increased from 12 categories to 23 since 2000.
cards.sub: Administrative ethnicity is becoming more granular, not less — the opposite of the Bangsa Malaysia trajectory.

cards.big: 72% of Malaysians aged 18-30 identify as 'Malaysian first' in surveys.
cards.sub: Youth sentiment aligns with Bangsa Malaysia. Policy has moved in the opposite direction.

cards.big: A national aspiration that disappears from national plans was not achieved. It was abandoned.
Abandoning Bangsa Malaysia without replacing it with an equally ambitious integrationist vision leaves a vacuum that ethnic politics will fill. The alternative to a shared identity is competing ones.
